minutes_briefs.title: New rules for Metro Vancouver Transit Police take effect
minutes_briefs.summary: The BC government has replaced three old regulations with one new regulation for the South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Police Service (also known as Metro Vancouver Transit Police). This new regulation sets out how the transit police service, its law enforcement unit (the officers who enforce laws), and its policing board (the group that oversees the police) are organized and run. The old rules from 2004 and 2022 are now cancelled.
minutes_briefs.action_required: No immediate action is required, but review your business's security or transit-related policies to ensure they align with the new police structure.

key_dates.label: New transit police regulation takes effect

minutes_briefs.source_body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 80/2026
Made under: Police Act
Published in: No. 9 – B.C. Regs. 77 to 82, May 19, 2026
Deposit date: May 11, 2026

B.C. Reg. 80/2026, deposited May 11, 2026, under thePOLICE ACT[sections 74, 74.1 and 74.2] andPOLICE AMENDMENT ACT, 2024[section 131]. Order in Council 187/2026, approved and ordered May 11, 2026. On the recommendation of the undersigned, the Lieutenant Governor, by and with the advice and consent of the Executive Council, orders that (a)	the attached South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Police Service Regulation is made, and (b)	the following are repealed: (i)	the South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Police Service Operations Regulation, B.C. Reg. 484/2004; (ii)	the South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Police Service Regulation, B.C. Reg. 454/2004; (iii)	the terms of the applications, attached to Order in Council 586/2022, in accordance with which terms the designated policing unit, the designated law enforcement unit and the designated boards referred to in Ministerial Order 339/2022 were established. — N. KRIEGER,Minister of Public Safety and Solicitor General; J. SUNNER,Presiding Member of the Executive Council. SOUTH COAST BRITISH COLUMBIATRANSPORTATION AUTHORITYPOLICE SERVICE REGULATION "transit law enforcement unit"means the designated law enforcement unit (a)	established by Ministerial Order 339/2022 and continued by section 129 (2)[transition – continuation of pre-existing designated law enforcement units and    boards]of thePolice Amendment Act, 2024, and (b)	known as the South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Police Service Designated Law Enforcement Unit; "transit police board"means the designated policing board described in section 2 (1) and (2); "transit police service"means the South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Police Service, also known as the Metro Vancouver Transit Police, consisting of both of the following: (b)	the transit law enforcement unit; "transit policing unit"means the designated policing unit (a)	established by Ministerial Order 339/2022 and continued by section 129 (1)[transition – continuation of pre-existing designated policing units and boards]of thePolice Amendment Act, 2024, and (b)	known as the South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Police Service Designated Policing Unit; "TransLink"has the same meaning as "authority" in section 1 (1) of theSouth Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Act; "transportation service region"has the same meaning as in section 1 (1) of theSouth Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Act.
